medical_history.chief_complaint: Direct admission for initiation of hemodialysis
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with history of hypertension, TIA, CRF, known 3.3 cm infrarenal AAA with thrombus admitted for placement of a tunneled hemodialysis line and initiation of hemodialysis. Developed acute on chronic renal failure with rapid worsening of creatinine to 8.4 today (baseline ~2). Patient declined hemodialysis previously but accepted today. Reports she does not desire hemodialysis.
medical_history.past_medical_history: 1) TIA 2) Hypertension 3) Hyperlipidemia 4) Chronic kidney disease, non-proteinuric 5) Diverticulosis 6) Depression 7) s/p CEA 8) 3.3 cm infrarenal AAA 9) s/p cholecystectomy 10) PVD 11) Recent admission for chronic abdominal pain due to infrarenal aneurysm and question of chronic dissection with thrombus of the infrarenal aorta.

physical_examination.general_examination: On admission: Vital signs: T: 98.9 BP: Not specified P: 50 RR: 18 SpO2: 99 %RA. General: Well-appearing, younger than stated age; HEENT: NCAT, + L carotid bruit, no JVD; Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm with II/VI systolic murmur heard best at the base; Lungs: CTAB; Abdomen: Soft and nontender, + bowel sounds; Extremities: no c/c/e, nontender, 2+ DP pulses b/l; Neurologic: Grossly normal, alert and oriented.
